100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 6, 1926, British Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin announced a significant reduction in the import duties on foreign textiles, aiming to bolster domestic industries amid economic challenges. In the United States, the Supreme Court upheld the decision to restrict the sale of alcoholic beverages in the ongoing era of Prohibition, igniting further debates on the effectiveness of the 18th Amendment. Meanwhile, political tensions escalated in Germany as the Weimar Republic faced increasing pressure from both leftist and rightist factions.
